text.title: go-error-handling-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for go-error-handling-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: fmt.Errorf("failed: %v", err) — formats with %v instead of %w.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Breaks errors.Is/As traversal. errorlint flags this. Replace %v with %w when err is the last argument.

failure-mode.description: if err == apperror.ErrNotFound { ... } — direct equality check.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Fails when err is wrapped. Use errors.Is(err, apperror.ErrNotFound). errorlint catches this.

failure-mode.description: log.Error("db failed", err); return err — logging then returning the same error up the stack.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each layer logs once, producing N alerts for one failure. Decide where the boundary is and log there only.

failure-mode.description: return errors.New("user not found") — bare ad-hoc error string in domain code.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cannot be matched with errors.Is across packages. Use a sentinel from apperror.
